content.problemStatement:
Halloween is approaching, and you plan to distribute sweets to the children visiting your house. You expect a total of MMM children.

You have NNN boxes of candies and NNN boxes of chocolates. The ithi^{th}ith candy box contains AiA_iAi​ candies, and the ithi^{th}ith chocolate box contains BiB_iBi​ chocolates.

Your task is to choose **exactly one** box of candies and **exactly one** box of chocolates.

You will then mix the selected candies and chocolates and distribute the combined sweets equally among the MMM children such that no sweet is left.

Each child should receive the same number of sweets, but the types of sweets do not need to be identical.

Count the number of ways to choose 111 box of candy and 111 box of chocolates to be able to evenly distribute the sweets among the MMM children.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM — the number of boxes and the number of children.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, denoting the number of candies in each box.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​, denoting the number of chocolates in each box.




Output Format
For each test case, output the number of ways of choosing 111 box of candy and 111 box of chocolates to evenly distribute the sweets among the MMM children.
constraints: The sum of NNN does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105 over all test cases.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There are 555 ways to select the boxes such that sweets can be evenly distributed:

Select candy box 111 and chocolate box 444 to have total of 2+13=152+13=152+13=15 sweets. This way we can distribute 333 sweets each.
Select candy box 222 and chocolate box 222 to have total of 8+12=208+12=208+12=20 sweets. This way we can distribute 444 sweets each.
Select candy box 333 and chocolate box 222 to have total of 3+12=153+12=153+12=15 sweets. This way we can distribute 333 sweets each.
Select candy box 444 and chocolate box 111 to have total of 10+5=1510+5=1510+5=15 sweets. This way we can distribute 333 sweets each.
Select candy box 444 and chocolate box 333 to have total of 10+15=2510+15=2510+15=25 sweets. This way we can distribute 555 sweets each.

Test case 222: Any candy box can be chosen along with any chocolate box.
Test case 333: Since we have only one candy box and one chocolate box, choosing them would result in total of 23+76=9923+76=9923+76=99 sweets which cannot be evenly distributed between 100100100 children.
